reason: The missing hints are for tools whose full names and content already appear verbatim in the s1 section body (the markdown table). Since this is a single-section file, section-level hint matching does not gate access to the content — the section is always included when the file is retrieved. An agent querying for any of these tool names would find the content via full-text/semantic matching on the section body itself. The hints gap has no meaningful effect on retrieval quality.

findings_assessment.justification: The four missing terms (リクエストテーブル登録用データ作成ツール/リクエストID, コードテーブル登録用データ出力ツール/コードテーブル, メッセージテーブル登録用データ作成ツール/メッセージテーブル, 整合性チェックツール/整合性チェック) are all present verbatim in the s1 section body text. Because toolbox-tool has only one section, hint-based filtering cannot exclude s1 — it is always returned when the file is selected. Therefore the missing hints do not reduce the agent's ability to surface this content. Phase E logs are empty, indicating no fix was attempted; the loop likely treated this as below the fix threshold given the content is accessible.
